findings.result: Real deployment data shown — 7 deployments with Name, Namespace, Ready, Image, Status, Age. Namespace filter works (tested 'voyager' filter: 5/5 deployments shown)
Real service data shown — 9 services with Name, Type, ClusterIP, Ports, Age. NOT a stub.
6 namespaces listed: default, ingress-nginx, kube-node-lease, kube-public, kube-system, voyager. All Active.
13 pods shown, grouped by namespace (ingress-nginx:1, kube-system:7, voyager:5). All Running.
No offline fallback banner visible — cluster IS online. Acceptable behavior. Banner likely only shows when cluster is offline.
48 events shown, sorted newest first (~27m ago). Live blue dot indicator visible. Pagination: 1-25 of 48.
Pod selector dropdown with 13 pods. Switching pods works — voyager-api shows real JSON API logs. Auto-scroll and line count controls present.
Graceful empty state: 'No autoscaling configured. Karpenter is not installed or has no NodePools configured for this cluster.' — correct for minikube.
'Collecting metrics data...' state shown with proper message. NOT a blank stub. Time range selectors and metric type toggles all present.

Cmd+K opens palette. 'Cluster Tabs' section shows all 10 tabs: Overview(1), Nodes(2), Pods(3), Deployments(4), Services(5), Namespaces(6), Events(7), Logs(8), Metrics(9), Autoscaling(—). Also shows Navigation, Clusters, Resources, Actions groups.
Keys 1-9 navigate to respective tabs (verified: key 1 → Overview, key 4 → Deployments). Command palette also opens simultaneously — minor UX quirk but navigation works.
[ navigates to previous tab (Deployments → Pods), ] navigates to next tab (Pods → Deployments). Works correctly.

HUNDREDS of 401 Unauthorized errors from tRPC background polling endpoints: clusters.list, health.status, presence.subscribe, presence.heartbeat, clusters.live, events.list, alerts.unacknowledgedCount, metrics.currentStats, presence.getOnlineUsers. Auth token not being properly maintained for subscription/polling connections. Plus ERR_INCOMPLETE_CHUNKED_ENCODING on presence.subscribe. UI still functions but backend is spamming retries.

Sidebar shows: Dashboard, Clusters, Alerts, Events, Logs, Settings. Expected per Phase 1 spec: Dashboard, Clusters, Alerts, AI Assistant, Dashboards, Settings. Events and Logs present instead of AI Assistant and Dashboards. The 6-item count matches but composition diverges from spec.
Sidebar shows CLUSTERS section with prod-cluster-eks and test-cluster-minikube as quick-links. /clusters/{id} navigation works correctly.
/settings/users ✅, /settings/teams ✅. Settings tabs: General, Users, Teams, Permissions, Webhooks, Feature Flags, Audit Log all present.
Breadcrumbs correct on all tested pages: Home > Clusters > test-cluster-minikube > [Tab], Home > Settings > Users, Home > Settings > Teams.

regressions.description: Sidebar shows Events+Logs instead of expected AI Assistant+Dashboards. Phase 1 spec says 6 items: Dashboard, Clusters, Alerts, AI Assistant, Dashboards, Settings. Current: Dashboard, Clusters, Alerts, Events, Logs, Settings.

Continuous 401 Unauthorized errors on all tRPC background endpoints. Session auth not maintained for subscription/polling connections. This is a backend auth regression that degrades performance and will exhaust clients.

checks: Deployments tab - real data with namespace filter
Services tab - real data, not stub
Namespaces tab - namespace list with status
Pods tab - pod data grouped by namespace
Pods tab - offline banner check (N/A, cluster online)
Events tab - live indicator, sorted newest first
Logs tab - pod selector, log output on pod switch
Metrics tab - collecting state, not blank stub
Command Palette - Cluster Tabs section with shortcuts
Sidebar 6 items check (count OK, composition differs)
Breadcrumbs on cluster detail and settings pages
failures: BUG-1: Hundreds of 401 Unauthorized console errors from tRPC background polling — auth not maintained for subscriptions (HIGH severity)
BUG-2: Sidebar shows Events+Logs instead of AI Assistant+Dashboards vs Phase 1 spec (MEDIUM severity — Phase 1 regression)
notes: Phase 2 feature implementation is excellent — all 10 cluster tabs have real data or appropriate empty states. Command palette, keyboard shortcuts ([, ], 1-9), and breadcrumbs all work correctly. The gate FAILS on: (1) high-severity 401 auth storm in browser console from background tRPC polling, (2) sidebar shows Events+Logs instead of AI Assistant+Dashboards vs Phase 1 spec. The 401s in particular indicate a backend auth regression that could affect real-time features under load.
